stories.headline: What was the Iran nuclear deal Trump dumped in search of ‘better’ terms?
stories.summary: Trump withdrew the US from the JCPOA in 2018, branding it 'the worst deal ever' and seeking stronger terms.

stories.headline: Qatar backs ceasefire as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad
stories.summary: Qatar has urged diplomacy on Tuesday as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks, warning prolonged tensions risk wider global fallout, particularly if disruption in the Strait of Hormuz affects energy flows and markets.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy announces Druzhba oil pipeline repaired and 'can resume operation'
stories.summary: The repair of the Druzhba pipeline paves the way for the adoption of the €90 billion loan for Ukraine and a new round of sanctions against Russia, both of which are currently vetoed by Hungary.

stories.headline: Israeli soldiers and settlers kill 11 Palestinians across Gaza, West Bank
stories.summary: In Hebron, a 16-year-old is struck by a vehicle escorting an Israeli minister and killed.

stories.headline: Trump expects ‘great deal’ with Iran amid uncertainty over peace talks

stories.headline: Trump says he opposes extending Iran ceasefire amid talks uncertainty
stories.summary: US president says Iran has 'no choice' but to show up to the negotiations in Pakistan and accept a 'great' deal.

stories.headline: Why is the EU under pressure to suspend its trade agreement with Israel?
stories.summary: Divided EU nations debate suspending Israel deal as pressure mounts over rights violations in Gaza and the West Bank.

stories.headline: Palestine weekly wrap: Coordinated attacks and evictions in Gaza, West Bank
stories.summary: The killings of Palestinians in Gaza and settler violence across the West Bank are both continuing to surge.

stories.headline: US Stocks Decline as Investors Mull Warsh Testimony, Iran
stories.summary: US stocks traded lower on Tuesday as investors pored over the latest corporate earnings while assessing the updates on the Iran ceasefire talks as well as Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

stories.headline: Treasuries Weaken Before Warsh Testifies for Fed Chair Role
stories.summary: Treasuries fell as strong economic data prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations ahead of testimony from Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh.

stories.headline: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
stories.summary: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

stories.headline: The Onion launches new effort to turn Infowars into parody website
stories.summary: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

stories.headline: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
stories.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

stories.headline: Wall Street Banks Flag Risks for Kenya’s Shilling as War Rages On
stories.summary: Kenya’s shilling is among the most vulnerable currencies in Africa, according to strategists at major banks, who predict further weakness as elevated oil prices pile pressure on the economy.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz: How a threat became a playbook
stories.summary: How the Strait of Hormuz became a system of control, shaping power and the global economy.

stories.headline: EU to boost jet fuel imports from the United States amid shortage fears
stories.summary: EU Transport Commissioner Apostolos Tzitzikostas has said there are no indications of jet fuel shortages, but stressed the importance of acting quickly and appropriately. The use of emergency stocks is under consideration "if real supply issues arise".

stories.headline: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.headline: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
stories.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

stories.headline: Apple’s smooth CEO swap is the ultimate corporate status symbol
stories.summary: There are two kinds of companies — those that can do CEO transitions without intense upheaval and those that can’t

stories.headline: Revolut aims for $200bn valuation in stock market listing

stories.headline: VW to export more China-made EVs in global sales drive
stories.summary: Strategy pivot coincides with slowdown and fierce competition in world’s largest automotive market

stories.headline: Rising Costs Threaten the Airline Boom
stories.summary: Airlines are walking a tightrope: strong demand and pricing power on one side, rising fuel and labor costs on the other. TD Cowen analyst Tom Fitzgerald joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why baggage fees are sticking, why government bailouts are unlikely, and how long airlines can keep passing costs to consumers before demand starts cracking. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: MUFG Bank Seeks More Acquisitions After $4.3 Billion India Deal
stories.summary: MUFG Bank Ltd. is looking for opportunities to make further acquisitions or investments in Asia and elsewhere, according to the new head of the lending unit at Japan’s largest banking group.

stories.headline: Trump Urges Judge Not to Let Dimon ‘Escape’ JPMorgan Lawsuit
stories.summary: Donald Trump’s lawyer said JPMorgan Chase & Co. Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon can’t “escape liability” for allegedly blacklisting the president after Jan. 6, 2021.

stories.headline: Qualcomm’s Best Run in Years Barely Puts a Dent in Brutal Selloff
stories.summary: By most objective measures, Qualcomm Inc. appears to be in the midst of a stock market heater.

stories.headline: Ukraine Willing to Delay Access to EU Benefits, Deputy PM Kachka Says
stories.summary: Ukraine is open to delay access to the EU Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) funding initially, however, it may become part of negotiations over the next seven-year budget, which would begin in 2034, Ukraine’s Deputy Prime Minister Taras Kachka says in an interview on Bloomberg TV. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Le Pen’s Energy Plan for France Called a ‘Disaster’ by Engie CEO
stories.summary: A plan by Marine Le Pen’s far-right party to scrap renewables and the energy regulator would be a disaster for France, the CEO of giant utility Engie SA said, in one of the sharpest rebukes from a business leader ahead of next year’s presidential election.

stories.headline: Looking inland: Portugal to boost regional tourism with €11 million projects
stories.summary: The projects will support nature, gastronomy, wellness and cultural tourism within Portugal’s inland, and attract visitors away from major tourist hotspots like Lisbon and Porto.

stories.headline: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
stories.summary: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.

stories.headline: Hot air balloon makes emergency landing in California backyard

stories.headline: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran

stories.headline: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Next Round of US-Iran Peace Talks
stories.summary: Gold declined as traders assessed prospects for deal to end the Iran war, while also monitoring Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing for clues about future US monetary policy.

stories.headline: House Ethics Panel Defends Handling of Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: In urging victims to step forward, the secretive panel in charge of investigating charges of misconduct by members of Congress acknowledged limits that could allow offenses to go unaddressed.

stories.headline: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon
stories.summary: The dramatic end to the Delaware Marathon occurred when the lead runner slowed in celebration before a trailing marathoner sprinted toward the finish line .

stories.summary: A rebound in oil drove stocks lower as traders awaited confirmation that Iran will join peace talks with the US before a ceasefire expires, with the two sides still at odds on key issues.

stories.headline: Kansas Mayor Who Voted Illegally Will Not Serve Time in Prison
stories.summary: Joe Ceballos, a green card holder who resigned as Coldwater’s mayor after being charged, said he did not know that he had to be a citizen to vote.

stories.summary: The rare move comes after recent scandals raised questions from lawmakers and others about how Congress handles investigations involving its members.

stories.headline: Rebel Wilson's claims against actress are 'malicious concoctions', Australian court hears
stories.summary: Wilson has been accused of defaming the star of her film The Deb over an incident involving a bath in Bondi.

stories.headline: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
stories.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.headline: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
stories.summary: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

stories.headline: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
stories.summary: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire

stories.headline: He made jazz under air raids - and built an Indian city's music scene
